---
title: "Setup — Posit Cloud and Student Workspace"
subtitle: "A 15-minute preflight before we begin"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

## Today we are using Posit Cloud

The workshop runs in a browser-based RStudio environment prepared by the instructor. Do not install packages during class unless the instructor asks you to; the template should already contain everything required for the core exercises.

::: {.callout-important}
## Work only in `student_work/`
The course website and lesson files are reference material. Your morning `.Rmd` file and cumulative Quarto report are `student_work/morning_report.Rmd` and `student_work/my_report.qmd`. That folder is a separate Quarto project, so rendering your report cannot trigger failures in the website, capstone instructions, or slide files.
:::

## Step 1 — Open your own project copy

1. Sign in to the instructor-provided Posit Cloud workspace.
2. Open the assigned workshop project.
3. Confirm that you can edit files. In the **Files** pane, open `student_work/my_report.qmd`.
4. Replace `YOUR NAME` in the YAML with your name and save.
5. Confirm that `student_work/morning_report.Rmd` is also present; you will edit it during the morning block.

If your change does not persist, you may be viewing the instructor template rather than your own writable copy. Raise your hand before continuing.

## Step 2 — Confirm Quarto is available

In the **Terminal** pane, run:

```bash
quarto --version
```

A version number should appear. Then run:

```bash
quarto check knitr
```

The check should find R and knitr. Some checks for tools we are not using today may be unavailable; that does not block the core workshop.

## Step 3 — Render the learner report

With `student_work/my_report.qmd` open, click **Render** or press `Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+K`.

A successful render shows:

- a title with your name,
- a short readiness message,
- a table reporting a mean of 5.5,
- and no error in an unrelated course file.

You can also render from the terminal without involving the website project:

```bash
cd student_work
quarto render my_report.qmd --to html
```

## Step 4 — Check the core R packages

Run this in the R console or in a temporary R chunk:

```{r}
#| label: check-core-packages
#| message: false

core <- c("rmarkdown", "knitr", "dplyr", "ggplot2")
advanced <- c("kableExtra", "DT", "broom", "patchwork", "plotly", "quarto")

check_packages <- function(x) {
  data.frame(
    package = x,
    installed = vapply(x, requireNamespace, logical(1), quietly = TRUE),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}

cat("Core packages:\n")
print(check_packages(core), row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nDemonstration/bonus packages:\n")
print(check_packages(advanced), row.names = FALSE)
```

Every **core** package should report `TRUE`. Missing demonstration/bonus packages do not prevent you from completing the required exercises.

## Step 5 — Know the recovery path

Your folder contains:

```text
student_work/
├── _quarto.yml
├── morning_report.Rmd
├── my_report.qmd
├── parameter_demo.qmd
├── data/workshop_biomarkers.csv
├── references.bib
├── nature.csl
└── checkpoints/
    ├── morning_report_complete.Rmd
    ├── bridge_converted.qmd
    ├── 01_yaml_complete.qmd
    ├── 02_table_complete.qmd
    ├── 03_figure_complete.qmd
    └── 04_crossrefs_citations_complete.qmd
```

If your report stops rendering, compare it with the most recent checkpoint rather than starting over.

## Data safety

Use only the supplied synthetic data during class unless the instructor has explicitly approved another dataset. Do not upload personally identifiable, protected, controlled, unpublished, or otherwise restricted data to the training workspace.

## Ready check

Before moving on, confirm all four statements:

- [ ] I am in my own writable project copy.
- [ ] `quarto --version` prints a version.
- [ ] `student_work/my_report.qmd` renders successfully.
- [ ] The four core R packages are installed.

Next, open `morning_rmarkdown/01_rmarkdown_basics.Rmd` when the instructor signals.
